items.title: /v/ Video Games: SNES
items.content_text: Out of every single console in existence, this one is just the best. The controller is amazing. The games are amazing. The graphics are amazing. The sound is amazing. The only issue I have is that it doesn't play NES carts, but it can play gameboy carts with the super gameboy. But still, amazing system. Easily the best.

items.title: /mu/ Music: Mr. Ono Lennon says the internet has degraded the value of music in people's liv…
items.content_text: Mr. Ono Lennon says the internet has degraded the value of music in people's lives, since it's all widely available basically for free. He's right. Yes, it's convenient, but it comes at a cost. It's like how taking pictures has changed. You would only bring a camera to special occasions, for commemorating something or documenting milestones. In the 1800s, people used to need a photographer to do it. Now we all have cameras with us at all times, and it's great and useful, but photos undeniably ho

items.title: /g/ Technology: Cultural Technology
items.content_text: ITT we post pieces of technology that aren't universal, items that are unique to various cultures. The chukudu is a remarkable, hand-built wooden vehicle that serves as a vital economic lifeline in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo, particularly around the city of Goma. Born out of necessity due to poor road infrastructure and the high cost of motorized transport, these robust, unmotorized scooters are ingeniously crafted from local wood and scrap metal, designed to carry massive load

items.title: /g/ Technology: AI facial recognition
items.content_text: Cops in my country are using ClearView AI to catch criminals. The AI has scraped social media for millions of pics of people's faces. How is that legal? Sure, I have an online presence. I put my photos online willingly. But I have never given consent for governments to steal my photos and use them for training material that is used in the legal system of all places. Actually, how is any AI scraping legal? Governments and companies have scraped 100% of the Internet and are using it to make Minori

items.title: /g/ Technology: >BPFDoor is a Linux based passive long-term backdoor used by China-based threat …
items.content_text: >BPFDoor is a Linux based passive long-term backdoor used by China-based threat actors. First seen in 2021, BPFDoor is named after its usage of Berkley Packet Filter (BPF) to execute single task instructions. BPFDoor supports multiple protocols for communicating with a C2 including TCP, UDP, and ICMP and can start local or reverse shells that bypass firewalls using iptables. https://attack.mitre.org/software/S 1161/ CommonSense™ me arse...

items.title: /mu/ Music: Songs with Epic Intros
items.content_text: I want to know if there are other songs like the following: "Foreplay/Longtime" by Boston "Funeral for a Friend/Love Lies" by Elton John "I Need a Lover" by John Mellencamp I distinctly like the following things about these: -feel like two separate pieces of music put together, but it works -the intro isnt single instruments and has the full band -no lyrics in the first half -very dynamic, almost prog writing in the intro half -catchy almost pop sensibilities in the 2nd half -prefer single track

items.content_text: Not to get too esoteric on you, but Barnett reminds me of Perry Como, who crooned exactly 100 hits in pre-rock 'n' roll 1943-1954 and kept going till 1960 and beyond, including a number-two 1955 cover of Gene and Eunice's "Ko Ko Mo" and the number-one 1956 teen-targeted "Hot Diggity (Dog Ziggity Boom)." Como hosted his long-running TV show in a cardigan, was married to the same woman for 65 years, and died of Alzheimer's complications in 2001, and though as a kid I thought he was boring, I now a

items.title: /g/ Technology: Causal Permeability Principle
items.content_text: Missing distinction in discussions about self-improving AI: A system can: - rewrite its own code - optimize its weights - generate new tools - improve its performance and still be trapped inside the rules that determine how it changes. Proposed criterion: P_C ≡ E*  C_rev where: E* = external reality / incoming causal information C_rev = mechanism that determines how the system modifies its own future modification process = causal reachability Claim: A genuinely open-ended AI system requires that

items.title: /mu/ Music: >The most classic of British synth-pop bands, and also one of the longest-lived …
items.content_text: >The most classic of British synth-pop bands, and also one of the longest-lived (but perhaps also the most overrated), Depeche Mode—featuring Dave Gahan (vocals), Andy Fletcher (keyboards), Vince Clarke (keyboards), and Martin Gore (keyboards)—emerged during the romantic and futuristic trends of the early 1980s. The style they invented is one of the least original of that era, but also one of the easiest to digest, often echoing Tamla soul and Merseybeat—the most conservative genres of the 1960s

items.title: /o/ Auto: Why weren't V8 Diesels popular in passenger cars?
items.content_text: AFAIK the only large scale production of V8 Diesels in passenger cars were the 3.6 and 4.4 TDV8/SDV8 engines used in the L322 and L405 Range Rover and Range Rover Sport >inb4 BMW, Mercedes and Audi had V8 diesels These sold nowhere near on the scale that the Land Rover V8 Diesel sold, and were niche, even at the time

items.title: /g/ Technology: The CMP 170HX seems like a scam
items.content_text: My understanding is these were always effective versions of higher cards that didn't meet quality. A practice nvidia has done forever. They then lock these defective chips in the bios. We've known this forever, but its been hard to unlock. Now some absolute Chad thundercock has cracked it, and we are seeing 40gb and unstable 64gbs on $200 cards. Just like the tesla p40 situation. But these chips are by their nature effective. They will not be stable otherwise nvidia would have sold them at the n

items.title: /o/ Auto: Financed POS as a brokie
items.content_text: Anyone else thought financing a car meant smooth sailing for half a decade only for the fucking thing to constantly need work that you can't really afford on top of the monthly payments and full coverage insurance? I have to take a shitbox with 300,000 miles to work instead. This is so goddamn retarded.

items.title: /tv/ TV & Film: Important news
items.content_text: Christopher Nolan's The Odyssey continues to dominate online discussions, but not all of the attention has been positive. The film's official trailer has now amassed 910,300 reported dislikes on YouTube, bringing it close to the one-million mark and highlighting the intense debate surrounding one of the most anticipated films in recent years. Despite the controversy, the trailer has attracted an impressive 11.59 million views, along with 106,021 likes and more than 46,700 comments, demonstrating
